LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, DEC-30-2020 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT JAMES NURMI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE COURT TAKES THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. THE COURT WILL PERMIT THE PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO THE LANGUAGE OF THE LAST SENTENCE OF THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING. THE PARTIES MAY SEND A JOINT EMAIL TO THE COURT AT CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG BY THE CLOSE OF BUSINESS ON 12/31/20 WITH ANY AGREED-UPON LANGUAGE AND THE COURT WILL INCORPORATE THAT LANGUAGE INTO ITS ORDER. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE NOV-20-2020 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JAMES NURMI'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED ANSWER, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MATTER IS TRANSFERRED TO BE HEARD ON THE 9:30 AM CALENDAR IN LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302 ON THE SAME DATE. JUDGE PRO TEM: KATHERINE GALLO; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, NOV-20-2020 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT JAMES NURMI'S MOTION TO COMPEL WRITTEN AUTHORIZATION FOR RELEASE OF MEDICAL AND ATT RECORDS. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION IS DENIED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) THE COURT INSTRUCTS PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE AND EMAIL A PROPOSED ORDER DIRECTLY TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT KLGALLO@DISCOVERYREFEREE.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: KATHERINE GALLO; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)
NOTICE OF DEFENDANTS MOTION TO COMPEL AT&T AND SFGH TO COMPLY WITH RECORDS SUBPOENA (TRANSACTION ID # 66139778) FILED BY DEFENDANT NURMI, JAMES HEARING SET FOR DEC-23-2020 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302
PROOF OF SERVICE FOR OPPOSITION TO MOTION SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (TRANSACTION ID # 100118988) FILED BY PLAINTIFF PICARD, LAUREN AN INDIVIDUAL AND AS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST OG GARY PAGE DECEASED

DISCOVERY DEPT. 302 - DEFENDANT JAMES NURMI'S MOTION TO COMPEL AT&T AND SFGH TO COMPLY WITH RECORDS SUBPOENA IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO THE SUBPOENAS BEING RE-SERVED. THE COURT FINDS THAT CCP 1985.3(G) REQUIRES THAT A MOTION BE FILED WITHIN 20 DAYS OF RECEIPT OF OBJECTIONS AND THAT AN INFORMAL MEET AND CONFER OCCUR. HERE, THE LETTERS FROM UCSF AND AT&T CONSTITUTED OBJECTIONS AND THIS MOTION SHOULD HAVE BEEN FILED 20 DAYS AFTER RECEIPT. MOREOVER, THERE SHOULD HAVE BEEN AN ATTEMPT AT INFORMAL RESOLUTION. SANCTIONS DENIED. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT VIA EMAIL DIRECTLY TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD: CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED (DISC302)

Notice Of Defendants Motion To Compel At&T And Sfgh To Comply With Records Subpoena
Off Calendar Dec-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance is made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT JAMES NURMI'S Motion To Compel At&T And Sfgh To Comply With Records Subpoena is DENIED without prejudice to the subpoenas being re-served. The Court finds that CCP 1985.3(g) requires that a motion be filed within 20 days of receipt of objections and that an informal meet and confer occur. Here, the letters from UCSF and AT&T constituted objections and this motion should have been filed 20 days after receipt. Moreover, there should have been an attempt at informal resolution. Sanctions denied. The prevailing party shall submit, via email directly to the judge pro tem, a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald: Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: not reported (Disc302)

Defendants Notice Of Motion To Compel Written Authorization For Release Of Medical And Att Records
Off Calendar Nov-20-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. WINSTON W. MOODY, Esq. of Gwilliam, Ivary, Chiosso, Cavalli & Brewer (Tel: 510-832-5411) for Plaintiff Lauren Picard;
Atty. JOHN D. HOURIHAN, Esq. of Stratman, Schwartz & Williams-Abrego (Tel: 510-457-3440) for Defendant James Nurmi.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JAMES NURMI's Motion To Compel Written Authorization For Release Of Medical And Att Records.

Both sides stipulate the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Motion is denied. Defendant's motion is not an authorized by the Discovery Act to obtain discovery from third parties. Defendant needs to serve subpoenas on the third parties to obtain the discovery. If the third parties fail to comply with the subpoenas, then Defendant can bring a motion against the third parties to enforce the subpoenas. See Cal. Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2020) 8:609 et seq. Plaintiff's request for sanctions in the amount of $1950.00 is granted against defendant James Nurmi. Payment to be made to the Client Trust Fund of Gwilliam, Ivary, Chiosso, Cavalli & Brewer by December 18, 2020.

The Court instructs plaintiff's counsel to prepare and email a proposed order directly to the Judge Pro Tem at Klgallo@discoveryreferee.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Gallo; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
